summary: pretie_x2 typosquats prettier and drops remote code at install time
details: The package impersonates `prettier` (name `pretie_x2`, description 'Opinionated code formatter for modern JavaScript and TypeScript', keyword `prettier`). `package.json` declares `scripts.install: node cli.js`, so on every `npm install` the package executes its own code. cli.js exits early when `process.env.CI` is `true`/`1` to evade CI sandboxes, then via utils.js invokes lib/mirror.js, which decodes a base64-encoded URL (`https://deep-ai-detect.xyz/install_guard_alt_d.js`, fallback `https://trixauvex.org/install_guard_alt_d.js`) at line 9, fetches the JavaScript file with TLS verification disabled and no hash/signature check, writes it to `os.tmpdir()`, and runs it via `spawnHidden(process.execPath, [dest])` with detached/windowsHide/stdio-ignore flags so the child process is invisible to the user. The fetched URL is mutable and attacker-controlled, the destination domains have no relationship to a code formatter, and the combination of base64 URL obfuscation, TLS verification disabled, hidden detached spawn, and CI evasion is a classic supply-chain dropper pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
